fact: The first official cricket Test match was played in 1877 between Australia and England at the Melbourne Cricket Ground (MCG). Australia won by 45 runs.
The Cricket World Cup was first held in 1975 in England, won by the West Indies, who defeated Australia in the final at Lord's.
India won the ICC Cricket World Cup in 2011 on home soil, defeating Sri Lanka in the final at Wankhede Stadium, Mumbai.
A standard cricket pitch is 22 yards (20.12 meters) long, and a Test match innings can last up to 5 days.
The Thomas Cup, established in 1948, is the premier international men's team badminton championship. India won its historic first title in 2022 by defeating Indonesia 3-0.
Badminton became an official Olympic sport at the 1992 Barcelona Games, with singles and doubles events for both men and women.
The shuttlecock, or 'birdie', traditionally has 16 goose feathers attached to a cork base and can travel over 300 km/h when smashed by elite players.
China has historically dominated Olympic badminton, winning the most gold medals across singles and doubles events since the sport's Olympic debut.
The FIFA World Cup was first held in 1930. Uruguay hosted and won the tournament, defeating Argentina 4-2 in the final match in Montevideo.
Brazil has won the FIFA World Cup a record five times: 1958, 1962, 1970, 1994, and 2002.
A standard football (soccer) match consists of two 45-minute halves, for a total of 90 minutes plus stoppage time.
Argentina won the 2022 FIFA World Cup in Qatar, defeating France on penalties after a 3-3 draw, with Lionel Messi lifting the trophy.
